entry.title: Re: Supplier Portal

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=x3I36iSzXCc

entry.title: Re: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP Build Process
entry.content: Yes, the purchase order is created in SAP S/4HANA in-premise and I am looking to use event from S/4HANA to trigger SAP Build Process. If same cost center is used in multiple line items of the purchase order, then it should aggregate the respective line items amount to be shown in the approval form. These each cost center head should get these approval workitems in parallel irrespective of each other.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP Build Process
entry.content: And cost center responsible person can be derived using an API from S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: No entry in table T503 for ALMessage no. PG301
entry.content:
Hi,

 

you have to maintain the V_503_ALL table through transaction SM30

 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: Drop Down not working in Mobile App - SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Can you post what the issue was in case it helps others?

entry.title: Re: Can not call Process Automation from Build Apps in preview (404)
entry.content:
This is all on trial?

Maybe share what was the cause of the original not working.

entry.title: Re: Drop Down not working in Mobile App - SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we are using an existing OData from our backend SAP system, the issue is with the entityset mapped to the dropdown field.
GET ENTITYSET method adds a blank record(need to go back and check why this was done) while returning values. This blank record is the root cause. In the web preview the blank entry can be seen in the drop down, but in mobile preview this blank entry is not visible and also interferes with selection.

Just putting it out there for discussion and if there is a patch required from SAP as the same component is behaving differently on web vs mobile preview.

 

@Dan_Wroblewski

entry.title: Re: Story Report Performance Management Data
entry.content:
Hi Arren,

 

I got this information by starting in the Query Designer with Form Template > Form > Goal (From Performance Management) > Goal (From Goal Management).

 

Hope this helps.

 

Best regards
Martin

entry.title: Opportunity - some additional reasons for status
entry.content:
Hi there, I need to add some additonal reasons for status changes for opportunities. Should be possible using scoping as a maintenance task, but I couldn't get it to work. Standard entries are made, system is  running in production mode for a while now. Maybe I have ignored a neccessary task. Couldn't find the error or what's missing in the scoping process. Maybe someone could be of advice here. Thank you!

Regards, Uwe

entry.title: Re: SAP Kernel release and Netweaver 7.X
entry.content:
Hi Isaias,

Thanks for sharing these information, help us a lot.

Regards.

🙂

entry.title: Re: Delete duplicate entries in ABAP CDS views using Table Function and SQL Window Function
entry.content:
with parameters

P_LIFNR1 : z007_lifnr

as

select from z007_tab2 as SO_J

left outer join z007_tab3 as SO_I

on SO_J.lifnr = SO_I.lifnr

right outer join z007_tab1 as SO_H

on SO_H.lifnr = SO_J.lifnr

{

key SO_J.lifnr,

key SO_I.ebeln as SO_EBELN,

key SO_I.ebelp as SO_EBELP,

 

SO_H.ekorg,

SO_H.ekgrp,

SO_H.bukrs,

SO_H.matnr,

SO_I.aedat,

SO_H.aenam,

SO_H.werks,

SO_H.lgort,

SO_I.menge,

SO_I.meins,

SO_I.netpr,

SO_I.waers,

SO_I.lfimg,

SO_I.lfdat,

SO_H.maktx

}

where

SO_H.lifnr = $parameters.P_LIFNR1

and SO_I.menge > SO_I.lfimg;


I'm getting the duplicates when the header in this case ebeln has 2 line items ,the are records repeated,

how to solve this issue?

entry.title: Re: CDS view giving duplicate entries
entry.content:
Hi,

did you get the answer for the issue as Iam getting the same error?

with parameters

P_LIFNR1 : z007_lifnr

as

select from z007_tab2 as SO_J

left outer join z007_tab3 as SO_I

on SO_J.lifnr = SO_I.lifnr

right outer join z007_tab1 as SO_H

on SO_H.lifnr = SO_J.lifnr

{

key SO_J.lifnr,

key SO_I.ebeln as SO_EBELN,

key SO_I.ebelp as SO_EBELP,

 

SO_H.ekorg,

SO_H.ekgrp,

SO_H.bukrs,

SO_H.matnr,

SO_I.aedat,

SO_H.aenam,

SO_H.werks,

SO_H.lgort,

SO_I.menge,

SO_I.meins,

SO_I.netpr,

SO_I.waers,

SO_I.lfimg,

SO_I.lfdat,

SO_H.maktx

}

where

SO_H.lifnr = $parameters.P_LIFNR1

and SO_I.menge > SO_I.lfimg;
while writing this cds view?

 

thank you,

entry.title: Re: Custom Tasks in Onboarding and Offboarding
entry.content:
@Abdul_Salam,

I was able to show fields according to the value of a previous field.

{
"type": "LEAF",
"scope": "#/properties/x-question03",
"expectedValue": "Yes"
}

But this only works if the field "question03" is a String.

If the filled field with "Yes" is a MDF picklist, this doesn't work and I want to know how to do it 😞

entry.title: How to merge DMS object (eg. PDF/JPEG) to an Adobe form output
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

How to merge DMS object (eg. PNG/JPEG) to an Adobe form output?

I am able to merge .PDF type documents from DMS to the adobe form output using class cl_rspo_pdf_merge but how to merge .PNG type documents?

Kindly help.

 

Thank you,

Priyanka.

entry.title: Slow in searching service call due to huge records
entry.content:
The service call module has about 150000  records.

When do the wild card search using * for the service call, at the service call list, if using the Copy Table function, the system will hang.

SAP > Service > Service Call > input * > Search > encounter performance issue > click filter > encounter performance issue again. Basically, due to the huge records. I tried to search by key word to narrow down the search then no performance issue.

Their is no performance issue with other modules. 

What is your suggestion? I thought SAP Business One HANA can handle huge records.... Should we refer the issue to SAP?

entry.title: Derive Funds Center against WBS Element in CJ20N
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a requirement to derive Funds Center on the basis of WBS Element in t-code CJ20N. There are two scenarios where Network activity is assigned to WBS in one case while not in other.

I have already defined the Function Module FMDT_READ_MD_WBS_ELEMENT where Source field POSID = WBS_ELEMENT and Target Field POSID = FUND_CENTER. I have also defined a derivation rule for WBS Element = Funds Center with respective values.

I have also defined the FM FMDT_READ_ORDER_NV_FROM_FMZUOB for Network activity.

I have already activated the FM Trace but in CJ20N when i click on save button then trace window does not appear at all (in both scenarios) so I am not sure if derivation strategy is working or not. Similarly I am not sure if there is any configuration required to enable FM account assignment derivation in FM-PS environment.

Following is the settings for Maintain Field Status for Assigning FM Account Assignments for PS:

Please guide how to resolve this issue. 

Note: System is already deriving Funds Center against Network activity in another scenario where PR is auto generating on Project. There FM Trace is populating and AVC check is also working properly.

Regards,

entry.title: how to bring record base on the max date
entry.content:
hello,

I have 2 queries

in query 1 I have distinct item and other columns

in query 2 I have

item, planner , user login, transaction date ,type

the item  repeating because of the transaction date 

my goal is:

 bring the planner,uselogin from query 2 to base on  the max(transactiondate) foreach(item) where(type=1) to query 1

I already merge both query on item

tried: if max(transactiondate) foreach(item) where(type=1)= transactiondate foreach(item)

then planner +' '+user login else ''

and even bring the max transaction date to query 1

but i cant bring the planner and the userlogin to query 1 its show me empty or context error 

any help 🙏 

thank you

Today I developed a project for the Excel file upload and download from the table using https://spreadsheet-importer.com/ .

But this spreadsheetimporter module not loading after deploying the app into CF, i am attaching the error snip.

Thank you

@Marian_Zeis , @Spreadsheet

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Wroclaw 2024 - Call for papers
entry.content: Hallo do i need to pre-register to attend?

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Single Packaged Product to Unpackage...
entry.content: Hello Christoph, Thank you for your detailed response and for seeking to understand our situation better. You are correct in your interpretation. We have two components, a battery and diesel, each requiring different UN numbers. These are assembled into a trench compactor, which has its own material number. Our objective is to sell the trench compactor and provide Dangerous Goods Information for both the battery and diesel separately. The challenge we are facing is that the system does not allow us to classify these components under a single packaged product classification, which impacts our ability to manage compliance effectively. Based on your advice, I will start an OSS dialog with SAP to seek further guidance on this issue. Thank you for your help and suggestions. Best wishes, Ajit Dude

entry.title: Invoice rejected in CIG with Error: not found in Partner Directory
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

For Document type: Invoice Details Request, for one supplier the invoice is being rejected with below error.

Error Description:
"Failed to resolve endpoint: xslt://pd:HCIOWNERPID:MAPPING_ANY_cXML_0000_InvoiceDetailRequest_AddOn_ARBCI30000_Invoice%20MAPPING_ANY_cXML_0000_InvoiceDetailRequest_AddOn_ARBCI10001_Invoice:Binary?output=bytes&saxon=true&saxonExtensionFunctions=%23xsltExtensionsV11&uriResolverFactory=%23xsltUriResolverFactory due to: Resource 'pd:HCIOWNERPID:MAPPING_ANY_cXML_0000_InvoiceDetailRequest_AddOn_ARBCI30000_Invoice MAPPING_ANY_cXML_0000_InvoiceDetailRequest_AddOn_ARBCI10001_Invoice:Binary' not found in Partner Directory".

Can anyone please help me with the resolution steps for this endpoint issue?

Thanks in Advance!

entry.title: Implement side-by-side extensibility with SAP Build Apps and SAP Process Automation in BTP onPremise
entry.content:
SAP Build is SAP’s low-code solution, aimed at enabling business users to effortlessly construct business applications. It integrates SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone into a cohesive development environment, introducing fresh innovations for swift app creation, process automation, and business site generation.

Scenario:
A business Partner(BP) fetched from S4Hana on-premise system. Then BP Tax no will change in BTP App . Then Approver will Approve or Reject. If Approver approved, then BP Tax no will update in backend.

Systems:
In this topic we used below Technologies and Systems.
1. SAP Build Apps
2. SAP Process Automation
3. SAP S4Hana On-Premise as backend

Steps:
1. API from S4HANA system should be exposed to get consumed.

2. Then build a App in BTP.

 

3. Get the Entity from Backend destination and bind it to Data of the App.

 

4. Build the App with 2 pages. First page will show the BP Header data in List format.


Second Page with Input field.

 

5. Create an Action to post the changes using API.

 

 

6. Create a Workflow using Business Process Automation in BTP.

In this simple workflow it will be trigger first from APP after Save button. Then it will go to the approver . If Approver approve, update will happen in BP.

7. Deploy the app in SAP Build Work Zone.

 


Test results:
1. Open the App from SAP Build App preview or SAP Build Work zone. The BP no and Tax Id fetched from backend on the first page.

 

 


2. After click on respective line, 2nd page will display. Give the Tax no for update.

 


3. After Save , Workflow will be trigger.

 

 

4. It will shown in the My Inbox App of approvers with updated Tax id.

 

5. If User approve, Update will be triggered from Workflow. Workflow will be completed.

 

6. BP will be updated with new Tax no.

 

 

This document has been prepared only a summary to showcase the side by side extensibility. There ae some configurations need to be done in both SAP Build Apps, BTP and SAP Build work zone.

entry.title: Unable to Activate Event Mesh Webhook Subscription
entry.content:
Good day SAP community,

I created a webhook on the 4th of June, last week Tuesday, however the status remains inactive with subscription Paused. I attempted refresh, clicked resume, however, it has been on Paused.

Has anyone experienced this before and solved this? Any solution to this because I recreated the webhook and still having same issue.
I look forward to all suggestions. Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Platform - My Inbox App Error
entry.content:
Hi,

Check if your user have the role: ProcessAutomationParticipant added.

After add this permison, please sign out and sign in again.

Regards

Enric

entry.title: BTP Toolbox: App to Backup & Restore HDI Containers
entry.content:
Give yourself full control over the data backups of your BTP Cloud Applications using this open source CAP Application.

If you are looking for an easy way to manage your HDI Container Backups, continue reading.

  • Setup Applications and HDI Containers that are running on your BTP landscape. This will give you a good overview and is the starting point for working with backups.
  • Perform Backups and Restores for your HDI Containers using a simple Fiori Elements UI.
  • Backups are stored on a BTP Objectstore (AWS S3).
  • Automate backup creation using BTP Scheduler, for example to create nightly backups for productive HDI Containers.
  • Perform Environment-Copies, for example from PROD to QAS to have good quality testdata.

The following image shows the HDI Containers and backups of a given CAP application that is running in our company.

From a technical perspective, this project will help you to understand:

  • How to connecto to a Hana Cloud DB using a dedicated "BACKUP" User from a CAP application using 
    @Sap/hana-client
  • Setup and configure Hana Cloud DB for exporting and importing backups to a Cloud Store.
  • Working with HDI Administration and HDI Container Groups.
  • Using BTP Scheduler service to call a protected API.
  • Creating longrunning background tasks from CAP using cds.spawn.
  • Sending asynchronous responses to BTP Scheduler Jobs for longrunning jobs (more than 15 seconds).
  • Working with AWS S3 Storage (BTP Objectstore), for example connecting with WinSCP and working with the @Aws-sdk/client-s3
  • Working with Fiori Elements to create a List Report with mulitple Object Pages, Custom Actions and more.

Feel free to use this project, submit pull requests, start discussion about ideas and if necessary, submit issues on Github (Community Support).

bkrencker/HDIBackupRestore (github.com)

Note: for more information please have a look at the README file on Github.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Excel Add-in issue - Go Button disabled
entry.content:
Hi , 

I am new to SAC and getting issue while connecting SAC with Excel addin .

Below is my SAC web url .

https://xxxxx-q.sa1.sapanalytics.cloud/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/33c23/app.html#/home

1. I configured trust orgins using sap help documentation.

2. I getting below error and go button disabled .

Please advise , whether I am missed any steps for tenant configuration ? or add any other steps for enable go button in sap sac excel .

Thanks,

Chanti .

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud outbound SOAP call SalesOrderBulkErrorLog_Out
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am creating sales order in S/4HANA Public Cloud from EDI message via CPI using SOAP message SalesOrderBulkRequest_In (SAP_COM_0288). This interface is working as expected. Link to business accelerator hub.

Now, I want to capture the errors in CPI through an outbound call from S/4HANA system via SalesOrderBulkErrorLog_Out under the same communication scenario. Link

I have created the communication user and enabled the outbound service in communication arrangement.

 

Configured an iFow in CPI with the endpoint specified in communication arrangement:

 

But, still the iflow is not triggered and 302 (redirect) error is displayed in message monitoring app.

 

Is there any specific setting I am missing? Could you please help?

Regards,

Anupam

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello from Austria, Vienna! I’m delighted to be part of the SAP community. 😊

entry.title: Re: Linking Project WBS to Timesheets in the "Manage My T...
entry.content: Since this is Public Cloud you will need to assign a resource in a Customer Project and the assignment will automatically show up in the timesheet app.

entry.title: HTML content is being display as plain text in HTML Content Focused Insights for SAP SOLMAN
entry.content:
Hi Guru,

When customizing an instance of the OCC dashboard and adding a gadget with the Renderer type set to HTML_RENDERER, I encounter an issue. Despite inserting HTML code into the HTML Content field in the query settings, the content appears as plain text.

However, when I try inserting text without HTML tags and format it with the predefined toolbar, it displays correctly.

I need assistance resolving this issue because the predefined toolbar is too restrictive and makes it difficult to create flexible HTML content.

I've followed the article provided below, but my SolMan system does not have an Option group labeled "HTML content" in the Query settings.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/focused-insights-2-0-sp04-part-ii/ba-p/13421689

Need your help to fix this issue.

entry.title: Re: Get User ID from User Email Address
entry.content:
Hi

Recently I too all the user's mail id and user name from SU10 click on the address tab and execute you will get the list of user available in client system from there we can export the list into spread sheet.

Regards,

Someshwaran U

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud outbound SOAP call SalesOrderBul...
entry.content: This is now resolved. Two communication arrangements were there for two different sender systems. One did not have the outbound messages endpoint configured correctly. The messages being triggered with the sender business system id that did not have the right endpoints. Hence, the issue was occurring.

entry.title: Can not link second e-mail address to my Universal ID
entry.content: I have added different e-mail addresses to my account but I can not link non of them to my universal ID as a second. what am I doing wrong?

entry.title: Re: Email Notification Functionality for PO in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: @SergioEscudero  : Did you check if a valid email address maintained? Also, if approvers are getting "My Inbox" Notifications or not, if yes then they should get an email as well.

entry.title: Driving enterprise transformation with “Clean Core strategy”-Business Process
entry.content:
In today’s world, customers have a nearly limitless array of services and products choose from. With this abundance of choice, customers now expect companies to consistently deliver the products or services –and if they don’t, the customer can always find another supplier. 53% of customers believe that in a few years, they will prefer interacting with AI-powered agents due to lack of human error.

What is “Clean Core” Strategy?

Enterprises must therefore be proactive about adapting a business core system with the technology to foster agility and flexibility to deliver value faster, more efficiently and adapt new business needs. RISE with SAP S/4 HANA methodology cultivates “Clean Core” strategy which enables core with the capabilities which can consume latest innovation and AI features, have smoother future upgrades.

So, in a Nutshell, “Core Core” refers to an ERP system in the latest release with cloud compliant extension and Integration, with standardize business process and quality, consistent data. It is not only important to keep the “Core” clean but also “Lean”. Here “Lean” refers to taking out the extensions that are not required so that upgrades will be easier and faster.

 ’CLEAN CORE’ is an approach to get and keep the organizations core enterprise management systems clean for better ‘maintainability’ and lower total cost of ownership (TCO). More information: ERP Clean Core Strategy | Move to cloud ERP and run on the core (sap.com)

What are Clean Core Dimensions?

The various dimensions of “Clean Core” Approach are –

  1. Business Process
  2. Extensibility
  3. Data
  4. Integration
  5. Operations

In this blog, I would profoundly explain main aspects and adherence principles for making business processes clean core compliant.

Business Process as a dimension of “Clean Core” dimension-

  1. Establish state-of-the-art business process governance, including proper requirement management and transparent organizational structures.
  • Proper requirement management includes a S4H_783 SAP_S4HANA_Cloud_EX_SSB_White_Paper.pdf or a similar deciding structure, a suitable methodology , and proper    documentation and tooling support such as the Requirement Management or the Focused Build  for SAP Solution Manager.
  • A Business Transformation Management structure is established and transparent across relevant organizations, including process owners and related contact persons.
  1. Use recommended SAP Reference Solution Architecture to leverage SAP solutions such as SAP S/4HANA and others.
  • The solution architecture can be based on the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework - SAP Community methodology. Supporting resources can be leveraged using SAP Signavio Process Explorer , SAP Signavio Process Manager - Best-practice process modeling, SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub - Process collaboration and transparency , and the SAP LeanIX | Enterprise Architecture Transformation.
  1. Implement respective SAP Best Practices where available and applicable.
  • SAP Best Practices can, in general, be compared to SAP Signavio Process Explorer, the Process Navigator - SAP for Me, SAP Signavio Process Manager, or SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub. Find more general guidance in the Transaction List | SAP Help Portal
  1. Tailor or enhance standard solutions from SAP only where competitive advantages can be created.
    • For non-differentiating processes, SAP standard is strictly applied; for differentiating processes, SAP standard solutions are tailored to business needs and enhanced where needed.
    • A focus on mandatory and key process variants helps avoid unnecessary customizing and process complexity.
    • For required extensions the recommendations for “clean extensions” are followed.
  2. Achieve business process management excellence by documenting processes and continuously monitoring process flow as well as process performance and efficiency.
  • Customer-specific process design is documented (SAP Signavio Process Manager and SAP Signavio Process   Collaboration Hub).
  • As-is process execution can be measured (SAP Signavio Process Insights - The fastest path to process improvements, SAP Signavio Process Intelligence - Unleash the power in your process data).
  • Business processes are efficiently executed based on measurable process performance indicators (SAP Signavio             Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence).

RISE with SAP- “Clean Core” success plan-

Roadmap to RISE with SAP S/4HANA has been upscaled with the ‘clean core’ success plan and quality gates at the end of each project phase. RISE customers get feedback and recommendations from SAP during the “clean core quality gates” at the end of each project phase which includes milestones and KPIs that customers should achieve to run a clean core. These tasks are based on best practices that we have gathered from thousands of successful RISE with SAP S/4HANA implementations. Each task comes with a clear description and is complimented with additional accelerators for further guidance. These gates ensure customers implement in alignment with best practice.

Roadmap Viewer (sap.com) allows immediate web browser access to the “RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition” project roadmap.

Outlook-

When it comes to “Clean Core” compliant business process, the goal here to wipe out inefficient process and leveraging SAP recommended best practices. It is agreed that companies can’t get rid of some of the business processes and extensions from day one of the implementation. Therefore, it would be essential to identify the business processes which are eligible for a “Clean Core” Journey and follow an iterative step-by-step approach for every new deployment.  

“Clean Core doesn’t happen overnight rather it’s a journey”.

Swadhinata Mudali 

Business Process Consultant

Digital Supply Chain Management -Cloud ERP Team

entry.title: Re: MVAL in SAP BPC 10.1 NW
entry.content: You are right, That is the reason i posted this seeking help. Is there any way to get 0Amount and 0QTY same time. That is we need all the Accounts with 0Amount and if 0QTY is available it should go to one account. We wanted all QTY in one Account.

entry.title: Re: Submissions for "SAP HANA Cloud multi-model" developer challenge: Week 1 - Setup
entry.content: My submission:

entry.title: CDS based Analytical oData for Fiori
entry.content:
During my developments I found that analytical oData services (or maybe to be more precise "analytical entitysets" within oData services) are needed quite often but it's not very well documented what options can be used to create them. In most of the official literature you will find oData generated on top of "analytical query" but this option is not supported in Restful ABAP programming model and makes some stuff (like adding additional entitysets in the same service) difficult.

As a first thing let's explain "analytical oData" on a very simple example and probably most common scenario: you have some kind of document item which has for example Supplier, Company Code, Fiscal Year, Amount with Currency code and Quantity with Unit. And you perhaps need to get the "sums" of amounts and quantities on different levels

  • per Suppliers
  • per Company Code and Fiscal Year combination
  • etc.

So your data is on the most granular level (document item) but you can pull aggregated data through odata service with totals on any level (or in other words for any combination of dimensions).

Typical UI consumers in Fiori / S/4HANA world for this would be:

  • List reports with Analytical tables (with total at the bottom and subtotals on groups) - built either with Fiori Elements or smarttable with analytical table or just analytical table itself
  • Cards in Overview Pages (perhaps having full total in header of the card and then subtotals in the chart below)
  • Analytical List Pages (with charts for visual filters, chart in the middle and analytical table at the bottom)
  • KPI tiles (generated through Manage KPIs and Reports apps)
  • and more

To enable this behaviour in oData service, the analytical entity must be annotated with sap:semantics="aggregate". Now getting to the actual core of this blog - how to achieve it.

There are different ways:

1) oldest option for SEGW based oData services - adding annotations in *MPC_EXT class, redefining *SET_QUERY_OPTIONS method in *DPC_EXT class etc. This is pre-ABAP programming model for Fiori (not applicable if you use CDS view as referenced data source) so won't go into details but a bit more about this here:

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw74/7.4.16/en-US/ef/34f3794e3b4287b5f4170b6d7e648a/frameset.htm

2) stack of CDS views where the main things are:

  • cube CDS view with header annotation 

 

@analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE

 

and measure fields (like Amount or Quantity) annotated with

 

@Aggregation.default: #SUM
//or obsolete way of the same
@DefaultAggregation: #SUM

 

and then on top of cube having "query CDS" which is exposed as oData service - achieved by

 

@analytics.query: true
@OData.publish: true

 

This is in my point of view most often mentioned way of doing it (e.g. in SAP Press Books for Fiori Elements). However it brings some difficulties - because it's using oData.publish: true it's not easily possible to add additional entitysets into the same service - for stuff like value helps. And in general when using the "analytical query CDS" some of the annotations and approaches you are used to from usual Fiori Elements / smart controls development won't be working as expected (e.g. adding value helps for filterbar, linking text and id fields)

More about this approach for example here:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/create-an-analytical-model-based-on-abap-cds-views/ba-p/13377500

3) is very similar as option 2 just using new CDS view entities. Basically instead of Analytics.query: true annotation your query CDS will be using "provider contract analytical_query" notation

 

define transient view entity ZC_CDS_QUERY
  provider contract analytical_query
  as projection on ZC_CDS_CUBE

 

Interestingly when I tried to add this CDS into Service Definition (expose it as RAP) it gives error that it's not supported (S/4 HANA 2023 FPS1). So when you want to expose it as oData service you end up again with adding annotation oData.publish: true into the query transient CDS view entity.

And you have same problems as with option 2.

4) Not very well documented in my point of view and most flexible and convenient + compatible with Restful ABAP programming model.

Don't create "query CDS", simply put annotation for Measure fields (Amount / Quantity) what we used to call "consumption CDS" - CDS that will be exposed as the entityset.

 

@Aggregation.default: #SUM

 

In the same CDS (or the underlying CDS views) you can put any other annotations that you are used to (e.g. ObjectModel.textElement or Consumption.valueHelpDefinition or UI.lineItem)

It doesn't matter if you use older dictionary based CDS views or new CDS view entitities. Works with both.

And then you can either go with ABAP programming model for Fiori and add it in SEGW as "referenced data source" - you will notice that CDS is ticked as "analytical". 

Or you can go with Restful ABAP programming model and add this CDS into Service Definition and expose it as RAP based oData service.

In both case you can mix the analytical and non-analytical entitysets (CDS views) in the same oData service and that means it's also possible to add transactional behaviour etc...

Based on my experience I would recommend using option 4 (without having analytical query) for:

  • Analytical List Pages, Overview Pages and list reports with analytical tables

The actual analytical query (options 2 or 3) is required only for:

  • KPI tiles done through Manage KPIs and Record - I noticed that oData services done through option 4 are not available to select as data source for the KPI - they must have analytical query in the background

It would be interesting to get some guidance from SAP or hearing about real development experience from the community. For something like ALP (Analytical list page) I saw only option 2 being used in tutorials but in practice option 4 makes development easier (and I actually saw standard ALPs using option 4). Is there any benefit of having analytical query? Is there perhaps difference in performance? I couldn't see any major difference. Interestingly we even noticed some issues in the ALP framework when we used option 3 (subtotals disappearing in the analytical table) and these issues are not there when we used option 4.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Platform Trial 1909 Available Now
entry.content:
@stephan_daub

entry.title: Re: Creating an SAP Account and getting started in the community
entry.content:
Hi David:
I am very interested in the digital skills initiative as a self-study candidate.  I am currently unemployed/not working, and want to take the opportunity to add an SAP certification to my skillset/credentials.

I couldn't figure out how to verify that I can receive private messages.  There is no explanation I can find anywhere that points me to that option. There are so many pages to go through.  Hopefully, you can respond to me with the needed invite, so I can start learning and working toward certification asap.  I am not sure, which path would be best, since I am a complete novice, but I feel that I would be most at home in the Finance area, since I have a Bachelors in Accounting.

I am a woman, and also tick most of the other boxes you listed in your post, such as: 

  • People who are physically disabled or neurodivergent
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts  
  • First generation college students
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

    Thank you kindly.

    Susanne

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
Hello @Vitaliy-R !
Thanks for this challenge!

I am also having an error in step 9!
Connection with my HANA DB:

entry.title: Embedded Analytics - ABAP CDS View: In a nutshell
entry.content:
To take advantage of SAP HANA for application development, SAP introduced a new data modelling infrastructure known as Core data services. With CDS, data models are defined and consumed on database server rather than on application server.

CSD Views are going to be default paradigm for BW datasources and embedded analytics in S/4.

What is CDS ?

1)CDS  is a new language (Open SQL)

2)Objects (CDS Views, CDS Tables, CDS Functions)

Combination of Language and Objects together is called CDS.

 

What is CDS Views ?

1)ABAP CDS Views:- Provided by SAP NetWeaver ABAP in combination with HANA.

2)HANA CDS Views:- Provided by SAP HANA XS/XSA application server.

 

What is ABAP CDS Views ?

1.Select Query on tables  [ Can also be select query on CDS view.]

2.Based on new ABAP OpenSQL syntax.

3.Achieves code push down to Database [HANA or other].

4.Embedded analytics = Set of CDS Views. [Previously called HANA Live (CLVs) are depreciated and taken over by CDS Views]

5.Semantic – Annotations(@). [Gives Properties to CDS Views].

6.Association – Joins on Demand.

7.Expressions [Limited in normal DB Views]

8.Parameterized[Input Parameter].

9.No need to activate any BI content and is a virtual data model.

 

Limitations in ABAP Dictionary Views

Due to certain limitation in ABAP view, the CDS Views comes in picture.

Below are the limitation :-

1.Could not write complex expression when creating a DB views

2.Union ALL were not allowed.

3.Inner Join and simple selection were only possible.

4.Calculation expression, aggregation and grouping were not possible

5.Nested Views(view inside view) is not possible.

CDS Views will be used to Overcome the limitation and to push down the data into the database to use the HANA DB capability.

 

CDS (Core Data Services)

Core data services are the collection of domain-specific language and services for defining and consuming semantically rich models.

DDL (Data Definition language):- Model and retrieve data on a semantic level higher than SQL. Ex: Create CDS Views, tables and extend the CDS Views

QL(Query Language):- To consume in ABAP code. (Ex:- Select* from CDS views).

DCL (Data Control Language):- Define Auth. For CDS Views.

It is tightly integrated with Transport layer as it is an ABAP entity.

 

CDS related Objects

DDL Source:- Dictionary Object, contains definition of SQL View and CDS View. Write your code here. Upon activation below two Objects will be generated SQL view and CDS View, neither of them can be edited directly.

SQL View:- Dictionary Object.(Can be seen in SE11). Representative of database Object. Do not create anything on top of SQL View

CDS View Entity:- The Actual CDS View. Not a Dictionary Object. Select * will happen on this Object.

SQL View VS CDS View

 

 

 

Data Definition

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Creating CDS related Objects

The name of the CDS entity is specified after the DEFINE VIEW statement. The maximum length is 30 characters.

It is recommended (not technical necessary), that the name of the DDL source and the name of the CDS View are identical[Best Practice].

The name of the SQL View is specified after the Annotation @AbapCatalog.sqlViewName. The maximum length is 16 characters(just like any dictionary view).

It has to be different from the name of the CDS Entity.

 DDL Source

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

CDS View Syntax Example

CDS View Syntax

 

View Level and Field Level Annotation

View Level and Field Level Annotation

 

Creating ABAP CDS View

Creating ABAP CDS View

CDS view belongs to three category

Basic view :- We don’t use directly. [@VDM.viewType: #BASIC] These are used to bring data directly from the physical tables in the database.

Composite View:- Used in Reporting Gives the combination of dimension and facts. [@VDM.viewType:#COMPOSITE]. this type of view is used to link basic views together through associations (like SQL joins between views). They are then used by the consumption views.

Consumption View:-Used in reporting. Actual Consumer [@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION]. These views allow you to create a specific query from a “composite” view. This is the only type of view that can be exposed to the OData or transient service to be consumed for reporting.

Used in BW, ODATA

Standard CDS Views

 

Architecture of Virtual Data Model

Architecture of VDM

 

Dependency Analyzer

The Dependency Analyzer provides you with an overview of the complexity of the CDS entity with regards to the SQL definition.

This means, you can see which database tables are used by your query to select data from the database and you can easily identify redundant joins which might negatively affect the performance of your query.

The Dependency Analyzer recursively analyzes the data sources and also displays the indirect dependencies to other database objects in the SQL Dependency Tree tab.

Dependency Tree

 

BW Side Modelling

Creation of Datasource

The Datasource for Standard CDS view are present in BW system (unlike standard datasource which reside in source side).

Not all standard extractors are released for CDS views and SAP will release them gradually.

https://www.sap.com/documents/2017/06/4e8689f9-c17c-0010-82c7-eda71af511fa.html

Creation of Datasource on CDS view to consume it in BW is done by the below in BW side.

Source system: [ODQ_CDS] ODP-ABAP CDS Views.

ODQ Datasource

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

The SQL View Name of the CDS View will be the name of the Operational Data Provider of BW datasource.

Annotations

Annotations(@) It Gives Properties to CDS Views to interact with the BW , Fiori, ODATA etc.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName

@OData.publish: true

@analytics.dataCategory: #FACT

@analytics.dataCategory: #CUBE

 

To enable the CDS view to be ready for extraction.

@analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true.

@Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true

Annotations

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Datasource Property

The BW datasource can either be Full-Load or Overwrite Delta. It means this is After-Image only.

This datasource will not be visible in ODQMON(T-Code).

Datasource Property

To have delta property in datasource additional annotations are required.

Annotation based delta datasource

@analytics.dataExtraction.delta.byElement.name: ‘lastChangedAt’.

@analytics.dataExtraction.delta.byElement.detectDeletedRecords: true

@analytics.dataExtraction.delta.byElement.maxDelayInSeconds: 300

Datasource Property- Delta

This datasource will be visible in ODQMON T-Code.

 

CDS View Performance Guidelines

1.Use left outer join instead of Inner join.

2.No Join on Calculated column.

3.Join preferably on Key fields.

4.Use Parameters and where condition as much as possible.

5.Take only required columns.

6.Use Performance related annotations wherever possible.

Ex:- @ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality

   @ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass

   @ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory

Reference: -

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw75/7.5.5/en-US/be/dc1723e35244e188c5a44a5f4f8340/content.htm?no_cache=true

https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw75/7.5.5/en-US/7c/078765ec6d4e6b88b71bdaf8a2bd9f/content.htm?no_cache=true#:~:text=A%20CDS%20view%20is%20defined,Dictionary%20tables%20or%20Dictionary%20views.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/abap-core-data-services-introduction-abap-cds-view/ba-p/13320992

entry.title: Activation errors while extending Material number length in BW
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Our client is upgrading their ECC to S/4HANA and in the process enabled the extended material number functionality in S/4HANA. The material number length is now extended to CHAR 40.

As a result, the material length need to be extended to CHAR 40 in SAP BW system to match with S/4HANA material length. We are doing a PoC in BW sandbox and followed the process mentioned in SAP Note 2422224, Scenario 2 (<= 40 CHAR) to extend  0MATERIAL length in BW environment. Our BW is on version 750, SP 25.  As per the note, the prior step is completed of maintaining table RSTMCNV in BW with same entry as table TMCNV in S/4HANA. 

Next step we try to change the 0MATERIAL length from 18 to 40 and conversion exit from MATN1 to MATNB. While trying to activate the infoobject we are encountering lots of errors with structural change at field level for dependent tables as below. It shows around 197 tables have structural impact at field and need conversion, moreover 0MATERIAL is not getting activated. If we adjust the table structure via SE14 the material field is going back to CHAR 18.

 

Structure change at field level (convert table xxxxxxxx)

Can you please advise if anyone has successfully able to achieve the 0MATERIAL length changed to 40 with data available in the BW system. Please suggest any best approach to follow to convert material length in BW for this scenario.

Thanks

entry.title: stop return s.o to be created when returned quantity equal to or bigger than the original billing
entry.content: Stop the process / creation of return sales order with billing reference in case of the returned quantity in return sales order equal to or bigger than the quantity of original billing in SD .

entry.title: Integrating Abby Vantage to SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am looking for sap connectors for Abby vantage to integrate with SAP CPI. Does anyone know or have any leads?

Thanks,

Prasanthi

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule
entry.content:
@AmanJain , and all, please

  • Come to the BTP Trial homepage at Home - Trial Home - SAP BTP Cockpit (ondemand.com),  
  • Click or tap on button "Go To Your Trial Account"
  • On the left hand side you should find a bar with items. The third item should read "Boosters". Klick or tap it.
  • One of the tiles that pops up to the right should read "Get Started with SAP Build Apps - Detailed Account Setup" with a "Start" command. Press the Start command. 
  • The system will perform a few prerequisite checks and let you know you don't have a custom identity provider (click on the "information" button to learn what's going to happen instead). Then click 'Next'.
  • Either a new subaccount will be created or you can have the booster add to an existing subaccount.
  • Once the subaccount is set up, you may want to try some of the tutorials.

entry.title: Re: Report for Creation and Change of master data History...
entry.content:
Hello Krishna

 

Configuration Change History is a report showing the change history of business configuration. If you want to check other report, please go to the related work center and check the report view.

For example, if you want to check who and when a supplier is created, you can go to Business Partner Data - Reports or Payables - Reports. There you will find a report called "Supplier Detail". In it, you can check who and when a supplier is created. 

When you go to the report view of a work center, all the reports related to this work center will show. If you do not find the desired one, it implies that currently such data is not supported in report.

 

Best Regards,

Cariford

entry.title: Re: How to merge DMS object (eg. PDF/JPEG) to an Adobe fo...
entry.content: Resolved by myself.

Trying to find any information or documentation on using this process for Production Orders, I have built up using ProductionOrderConfirmation but there is no action to post changes in quantity etc. Can anyone assist?

entry.title: Re: OData service unable to pass MD parameter
entry.content: The trick I used is not using an ODATA service and instead building odata queries in the Business Analytics work center. This video (first 20 minutes) is the best way to manage analytics in Power BI. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=I67Et6PW8WM, I then use power query to filter and maintain the data I need. I find the ODATA service for Power BI to be not worth using.

entry.title: Re: Service Quote Object Lock error
entry.content: Hello May, Kindly create a case to SAP support team. So we can further reproduce the confirm the root cause. Thanks, Adie

entry.title: Re: Usage and differences between MRS , RSH and FSM
entry.content: Thanks a lot for the explanation

entry.title: Re: MVAL in SAP BPC 10.1 NW
entry.content:
Hi,

  • Dimension =*MVAL(keyfiguretype1|dim_mem1||keyfiguretype2|dim_mem2||keyfiguretype3|dim_mem3)

You can use different key figures when using the same MVAL. Just play around with it. 

Thanks,

Bennie

entry.title: Re: Service Quote Object Lock error
entry.content:
Hello Adie,

 

We already raised incident around last two months ago. 

SAP support team checked it and suggested as below.

[We would recommend saving the first service quote before creating a new service quote. Kindly ask the users to save the service quote, immediately after copying or creating a new service quote and then make changes.]

But it is a bit difficult to suggest this workaround to users, so I am asking around how other partners dealing with it.

 

Regards,

May

entry.title: IDOC Interface to send GR and Inbound info from S4 to external system
entry.content:
Hi team There is a requirement where in need to generate an Interface with IDOC (WMMBXY) with Synchronous type that whenever Goods receipt is completed in EWM the same will update in S4 and the same information need to be sent to external system each time.

The info will include Po number, GR date, Value of product and Quantity received. An interface needs to be created from S4 to external system with IDOC to achieve this.

 

I am new EWM please let me the detailed steps to create an interface to meet the above requirement.

 

Thanks in Advance

Hithesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Excel Add-in issue - Go Button di...
entry.content:
Hi,

Tenants of the other domains, for example, sapanalytics.cloud or hanacloudservices.cloud.sap, must be manually configured by the Microsoft365 administrator.

Please follow the steps as mentioned in the below document.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD_OFFICE/2b73edbc8f8f4189a36bc7a2e038185c/db2f874261824d63adb3ca198f65ce55.html?locale=en-US

Thanks,

Basavaraj

entry.title: Re: Corporate applicants to the digital skills initiative: Tell us your story!
entry.content:
Hi David

First of all thanks for this digital skills initiative by SAP Learning.
I am interested in taking this learning opportunity.

Here are my details ,
Name : Mohana Priya
Location : India
Experience : Have prior SAP experience - 15 + yrs
Technology :SAP PI ,ABAP
Currently : Not employed with 2 + years of career break due to personal / family situation and kids

I fall under category Women .(Unemployed currently due to Career - break )

As i was in a career break for past 2 + years , i am lagging in technologies that i have worked ,
now i am planning to get back to work due to family situations and needs ,
so i am interested in upskilling with latest technology updates and also to explore
more in SAP relevant for my past work experience .This will help me in getting a good job opportunity .

Requesting you to consider this and so the needful , thanks in advance .

Thanks & Regards
Mohana

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CREATE1 with Invoice Plan (SE 771)
entry.content:
The 'Price Per Unit' logic was missing on CL_MMPUR_IV_PLAN~CHECK_IV_PLAN_PARTIAL(called by BAPI_PO_CREATE1)

I think it's a program bug, and you need to request SAP notes.

lv_po_total = my_po_data-net_value * my_po_data-net_quantity.
> There's no 'Price Per Unit' in my_po_data sturcture.

Plus, When modifying the invoice plan in ME21N or ME22N, a separate program check logic is performed instead of the above method.(Include: LV60FF0F - Subroutine: fplt_fakwr_ermitteln)

Thanks,

Seokwon Jeon.

entry.title: Pin to Home option not available in SAC ODE mode
entry.content:
Hi All,

I know that Pin to Home option isn't available in the SAC ode mode, and the Widget appears blank.

SAP had advised in the following KBA, that it will be replaced by the Watchlist option but SAP doesn't have any defined timelines for this option to be available 

3439482 - "Pin to Home" table widget on Home Page shows blank if table belongs to an Optimized Design Experience story (sap.com)

I have also read the blog , which talks about the limitation and restriction that comes with ODE

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sac-optimized-view-mode-pin-to-home-of-a-story-widget-not-available/qaq-p/12694026

 

But I would really like to know , if anyone has found any work around for this. 

Any help is appreciated. Thanks

Regards,

Zeenat S

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing Interactive Value Journeys (IVJs)

entry.title: Can we add additional Matrix Manager to a single employee?
entry.content:
Hello,

We are synchronizing EP from EC every 5 minutes.  And matrix manager information is synchronized with Job Relationship portlet data. I would like to register more than one matrix manager and use it in form route map. I imported the basic information with two matrix manager in the matrix manger field formatted 'USER ID|USER ID'  After then I download the user data file again, bit the matrix manager field is not changed. It contained only one matrix manager.

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT PROGRAM with export-import internal tables issue
entry.content:
Ok..I can see that its NOT working.

I changed the code to have a parameter on sel screen with :

Parameters : p_itab type string_table no-display.

SUBMIT  Zmyprog** WITH p_tab = p_tab.

This is passing the data from SUBMIT to p_tab.

But p_tab holds plain string data type. How do i break this p_itab to hold 10 different columns like my  itab_out2?

entry.title: Re: Subscription Failed: SAP CAP Multitenant application.
entry.content: Hi Syed, Can you please provide the content of your .cdsrc.json file or the CDS configuration from your package.json to better analyze the error?

entry.title: How to delete components in a planned order manually?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a way to remove components in planned order level?

In "Component overview" mode I can only change existing BOM and in "Collective Entry" mode only add new components.

How I can remove a component from planned order?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete components in a planned order manually?
entry.content:
Hi,

You can delete the components of a planned orde in Change Planned Order app in the following way:

  1. Go to the Component Overview screen.
  2. Select a line of component.
  3. Click on "Edit -> Delete". 

BR
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Syntax Error in GRC Dev standard code
entry.content:
Hi Luzkysainz,

what SP level do you have, and where the syntax error is explicitly coming? Can you describe some more details from the dump? If you can, I rather suggest to open a Support Case.

Kind regards,
Mate

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Live data connection to SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content: Thanks for identifying it. the correct link is Availability of Live Connection from SAP SuccessFactors to SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Help Portal. I will have this updated soon

entry.title: Re: Use all array values as dimension filter
entry.content:
Hello, 

something like that. You must fill your timeSlot array with push() and only after the end of loop you must use the setdimensionfilter. I hope it helps.

 

function onInitialization() : void
var currentDate = new Date(Date.now());
var currentMonth = currentDate.getMonth();
var timeSlot = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);

chartCompare.getDataSource().removeDimensionFilter("0CALMONTH2");

for (var i = 1; i<=currentMonth; i++)
{
 timeSlot.push("0"+i.toString());

 }

chartCompare.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("0CALMONTH2", timeSlot);

entry.content:
Hi CoronAF

Can you create a support ticket for the issue?

Hope it helps.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: Good to know: Firefighter Session related changes from Access Control Service Pack 21
entry.content:
Dear Jason,

I suspect some missing authorization (in decentralized environment you need to check both the RFC and the Firefighter User as well, as some authorization need to be assigned directly to the Firefighter User). If you get Unknown Error during logon then after you have Note 3443975 in your system you can check SLG for the exact error message. Can you share the Case number?

Best Regards, Mate

Andi

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT PROGRAM with export-import internal tables issue
entry.content: You can pass any type of variable through a program parameter (internal table and so on, except references) by defining it as NO-DISPLAY

entry.title: Re: Kyma Integration with SAP Cloud Logging. Part 1: Introduction and shipping Logs
entry.content:
Hi Gaurav,

We have completed the setup for "traces, metrics & logs".
As suggested, we began with the "dev" license, but it appears to retain the data for only a week depending on size.

Consequently, I am curious about how we can monitor the usage of storage space and memory, even if we opt for a larger capacity option (standard license).

Thank you for your guidance.

Regards,
Sarbajeet

entry.title: Re: how to copy a flavor from one t-code to another t-code?
entry.content:
I would suggest using Templates or Themes because copying (as Tamas suggested) to another transaction with totally different screens and fields is not usable. Usually, this copying is used for similar transactions such as MM01, MM02, MM03 that have the same or similar objects.

I am using Themes to present the same filing throughout the different transactions.

entry.title: Adapt your app to SAPUI5 2.x - Make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 product owners and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise to directly address real-world challenges from your custom development projects.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how applications are adapted to SAPUI5 2.x

SAPUI5 is SAP’s JavaScript framework and UI library for building cross-platform, enterprise-grade Web applications. SAPUI5 has been a solid foundation for enterprise apps for more than a decade and is still in its first major version. Despite its continuous evolution and the dedicated SAPUI5 modularization project from a few years ago, SAPUI5 has remained backward compatible. However, with new requirements, such as the demand for strict Content Security Policy, as well as the evolution of major browsers (e.g. the introduction of new technologies), SAPUI5 needs to adapt. SAPUI5 is preparing for the future by leaving behind outdated concepts like synchronous network requests.

You now have the chance to understand the impact of SAPUI5 2.x on SAP’s applications, on your own applications, and on the system landscape. In this CEI project you will receive guidance, best practices, and tool support to adapt your own custom apps to SAPUI5 2.x. (If you like you can already have a look at similar details for OpenUI5 in Introducing OpenUI5 2.x.)

Project Goals and Activities

  • Validate concepts for moving existing SAPUI5 1.x apps to SAPUI5 2.x such as the necessary code changes and tool support & documentation on the code adaptation
  • Identify existing pain points
  • Gather prioritized requirements for the SAPUI5 road map with regards to new major versions

Format of Activities, Estimated Effort

Participants need to be available in the August/September 2024 timeframe for virtual sessions. Estimated effort: approx. 10h for own development activities + 6h for virtual calls:

  • Initial call (1h) in mid-August 2024 to outline the project and explain the transition to SAPUI5 2.x
  • 3 weekly calls (1h) for Q&A and feedback in August / early September
  • A final feedback call (1h) mid September to collect & prioritize feedback
  • Closing call (1h) in September


Prerequisites

This is your chance to give feedback on all aspects of moving to SAPUI5 2.x and influence the direction of the SAPUI5 road map. Join this project if

  • you are working in development projects and are familiar with SAPUI5 code
  • you have custom SAPUI5 1.x apps with freestyle SAPUI5 code, which you can adapt to SAPUI5 2.x. (This can be freestyle SAPUI5 apps or SAP Fiori elements apps with extensions / custom page.)
  • you are using your SAP Business Application Studio / VS Code environment with SAP Fiori tools.
Register for the CEI project 'SAPUI5 2.x' from June 10 - July 11, 2024.

Once the registration process is finalized, the project team invites participants for the initial call to go through the project scope and timelines.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: I recently moved to a Macbook Pro 16" at work and love it (I use Macs at home), but the Java GUI experience is currently crap. Please add me to the beta program for Java GUI 8.10 or post a link here where we can register our interest. PLEASE.........!!!!

entry.title: Pass URL-Filter on BW Live Hierarchy Nodes from Story to Story
entry.content:
Introduction:

Currently it is not possible to pass an URL-Filter on Hierarchy Nodes for BW-Live Models with the standard options.
In this blog I´ll show how this can be accomplished with some simple scripting and story setup.

Use Case:

There is one story which contains input controls (or story filters) for specific dimensions with hierarchies and we would like to pass the selected members of the hierarchy (be it nodes or leafs) to another story where these values should be applied as respective filters again.

Solution:

The jump to the other story is bound to a specific action, e.g. click on a button. This Event will be used to derive the selected members and pass them as URL parameters.

The event contains the following script (example for two relevant dimensions on input controls):

 

 

var dim1_string = "";
//only retrieve selected members if not all selected
if(IC_Dim1.getInputControlDataSource().isAllMembersSelected() === false){
	var mem1 = IC_Dim1.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers(10000);
	for(var i=0;i<mem1.length;i++){
		if(mem1[i].id.startsWith("PSEUDO")){ //really selected members
			if(mem1[i].id.endsWith("-")){ //is a node
				dim1_string = dim1_string.concat("/0HIER_NODE!".concat(mem1[i].displayId));
			}
			else{ //is a leaf
				dim1_string = dim1_string.concat("/!".concat(mem1[i].displayId));
			}
		}
	}
}
	var p_dim1 = UrlParameter.create("p_dim1", dim1_string);

var dim2_string = "";
//only retrieve selected members if not all selected
if(IC_Dim2.getInputControlDataSource().isAllMembersSelected() === false){
	var mem2 = IC_Dim2.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers(100000);
	for(var j=0;j<mem2.length;j++){
		if(mem2[j].id.startsWith("PSEUDO")){ //really selected members
			if(mem2[j].id.endsWith("-")){ //is a node
				dim2_string = dim2_string.concat("/0HIER_NODE!".concat(mem2[j].displayId));
			}
			else{ //is a leaf
				dim2_string = dim2_string.concat("/!".concat(mem2[j].displayId));
			}
		}
	}
}
	var p_dim2 = UrlParameter.create("p_dim2", dim2_string);

	NavigationUtils.openStory("STORY_ID", "PAGE_ID", [p_dim1, p_dim2]);

 

 

Important to note is:

  • Also the implicitly selected members (aka leafs below a node) will be retieved from the "getActiveSelectedMembers" API. Hence, the allowed members need to be increased for most hierarchies (because the default is 100) - if multiple extremely big hierarchies are involved the performance and response time should be checked.
  • The "really" selected members are starting in "id" with "PSEUDO" and nodes are ending with "-"

     

  • This concept is used to find the relevant IDs and decide which enhancement needs to be made to fit the expected BW syntax - either "0HIER_NODE!" or just "!"
  • All members are concatenated into a string and seperated by "/" (other characters also possible, should not interfer with the expected member IDs)
  • These strings are handed over to an URL parameter and used in the NavigationUtils.openStory API

 

If the same should be done for story filters, the API "Application.getFileDataSource.getDimensionFilters" can be used which immediately only gives back the chosen nodes/leafs with the correct BW syntax. They can directly be concatenated into a string.

 

In the story on the receiving end these parameters are maintained as script variables:

E.g. in the onInit script, the parameters can be split into the members and applied to an input control (example of first dimension):

 

 

if(dim1.length>0){
var dim1_param = dim1.slice(1); //remove first char, is "/"
var dim1_arr = dim1_param.split("/");
IC_Dim1.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(dim1_arr);
}

 

 

With this the correct hierarchy filters from Story 1 are passed to Story 2.

Do not hesitate to reach out in case of questions.

entry.title: Carbon Footprint Calculation with IDM from Implico
entry.content:
Integrating with SAP's S4SCSD for Enhanced Sustainability 

Carbon Footprint Calculation with the IDM from Implico 

As industries worldwide prioritize sustainability and environmental responsibility, managing and reducing carbon emissions has become a critical aspect of logistics and transportation operations. Implico's IDM provides a robust solution designed to enhance these capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. This advanced functionality is tailored for industries such as oil, gas and chemicals, where efficient logistics and environmental compliance are paramount.  

Support for Sustainability Goals 

Aligning logistics operations with broader corporate sustainability goals is a key benefit of the IDM. By providing actionable insights and recommendations, the module can support companies in achieving significant reductions in carbon emissions. This alignment with sustainability objectives underscores the importance of integrating environmental considerations into logistics management within SAP S4SCSD. 

Here are some of the factors that make the calculation and management of carbon footprints easier with Implico's IDM. 

IDM as a Core Component of SAP S4SCSD 

With its functionality, Implico's IDM is an integral component of SAP's S4SCSD (Secondary Distribution Management for Oil & Gas) and boosts the users' requirements for a more detailed carbon footprint calculation. This integration ensures that secondary distribution operations - which include both bulk and bottled products - are not only efficient, but also environmentally responsible. 

Support for Emissions Calculation 

By using available data such as fuel consumption, fuel type or distances traveled, Implico's IDM functionality amplifies CO2 emission calculations for each trip or delivery within the SAP S4SCSD framework. In a highly competitive environment where companies are striving to understand and mitigate their environmental impact better, the level of accuracy provided by IDM is essential. 

Data Integration and Analysis 

IDM has the ability to collect data (e.g. telematics, GPS, fleet management) and seamlessly integrate with SAP S4SCSD. This integration can be used, for example, for historical analysis of carbon emissions, providing valuable insights for better decision making and strategic planning. The ability to analyze data from multiple sources within a cohesive system is a significant advantage for companies aiming to optimize their operations. 

Route Optimization 

One of the key features of IDM is its ability to optimize routes and loads, thereby minimizing travel distances and fuel consumption. By suggesting the shortest routes, the module helps reduce the carbon footprint of logistics operations. This functionality, integrated within SAP S4SCSD, ensures that logistics processes are both operationally efficient and environmentally sustainable. 

Data for detailed Reporting and Documentation 

IDM facilitates transparency and accountability in environmental reporting. By providing important data that can be used for documentation, audits and certifications, IDM supports SAP S4SCSD from the inside with its detailed reporting capability. This ensures transparency and accountability in environmental performance and supports companies to meet environmental regulations and standards.  

User-Friendly Interface 

Designed with a user-friendly interface, the IDM tool makes it easy for operators to enter data, generate reports, and access insights. Its seamless integration with SAP S4SCSD ensures a consistent and efficient user experience, minimizing the learning curve for operators and improving the overall user experience. 

Conclusion 

By incorporating these features, Implico's IDM not only improves logistics management, but also enables companies to take significant steps to reduce their environmental impact. This makes it an invaluable add-on for all organizations, especially those that are seriously committed to sustainability and responsible logistics operations.  

With IDM, Implico has developed a product that is natively integrated into SAP S4SCSD and closes the gap between sales and returns. Most previously manual processes in the reconciliation are now automated, allowing back-office operations to shift gradually from manual to automated processes. Since IDM has been used successfully for years within the supply chain of energy and natural resources, Implico is currently working intensively on a new, fully automated version which is in heavy development and ReadyToShip in 2024 already. 

The new version is scalable for every size of organization and provides fully automated trip and route planning. 

Implico's IDM provides a significant opportunity for market expansion and revenue growth while enhancing compliance requirements for environmental standards. 

Let's shape a sustainable future, together.

 

 

SAP S/4HANA SDM: This tool offers a cohesive approach to the trade and transport of liquids and half-solids, integrating seamlessly with the SAP system to streamline processes from demand forecasting to invoicing, thereby facilitating smoother operations in hydrocarbon product trading.

SAP S/4HANA RFNO: A dedicated solution for managing service station networks, it caters to various service station types, offering functionalities like wet goods management and loyalty card management, aiming to enhance the fuel retail experience.

Implico’s Quality Assurance Program for SAP solutions: Implico wants to ensure the implementation of SAP SDM/RFNO solutions is professional and error-free – not only when doing so ourselves but also when external companies are contracted to oversee implementation. This is why we also offer two partner services that are designed to supplement the implementation projects based on a methodological approach and with highly skilled consultants, both of which help to achieve the defined targets: the Partner Governance Service and the Partner Advisor Service.

entry.title: Re: What is the Better way for developing Reports in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Sai Bhargav,

I would recommend implementing SAP Fiori apps instead of reports, in particular SAP Fiori Elements. For example, you could check out SAP Fiori Overview and UI Development for more information.

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: BTP Toolbox: App to Backup & Restore HDI Containers
entry.content:
Hi  @Ben 

Great Ben for showing BTP Toolbox to the SAP Community. Keep exploring and share new blogs to all 🙂

entry.title: Tcode F-90 / App with the same funtionality in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello experts!

Could you please clarify to me if in SAP Public cloud there is some app same as tcode F-90 in onpremise implementation?.
My client does not have logistic module implementation but only FI module and I´m wondering if there is any app that can make aposting vendor against fixed asset?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Good to know: Firefighter Session related changes from Access Control Service Pack 21
entry.content: Your tips for better financial operations are very helpful. Using tools like sharepoint syntex can also improve document management and automation, making financial tasks even easier. Thanks for sharing these useful ideas.

entry.title: SBO Mail not working in New installation 10.0 FP2402
entry.content:
Hi there.

I have prepared a new server to migrate our installation from WS2012+SQL2016 and SBO 10.0 FP2208 to WS2022+SQL2019 to SBO 10.0 FP2402 and then FP2405.

The installation and migration process was all correct.

Problem: SboMail and alert system. They do not work.

I have reviewed installation notes, reinstalled, SAP notes and nothing solves the problem.

Sending emails from the server from sqlmail works ok (from the transaction notification in sap database as well).

Sending emails from the B1if also works correctly.

Only from SAP it is not possible.

The same mailbox and configuration everywhere.

It can also be sent from SAP and through Outlook, except for the alert system.

Checking the operating system event viewer, I have an error saying event 62, source SBO Mail. The component causing this event is not installed on the local computer or the installation is corrupted.

What could we be missing from the configuration?

What could we be missing in the installation?

The Windows service is working without errors and the system and database configurations are correct, without errors of any kind.

entry.title: Re: CAP: Consume External Service - Part 1
entry.content:
The URL provided for the edmx didn't work for me (probably the web service changed in time)

Finally I've found the edmx V4 it at https://services.odata.org/v4/northwind/northwind.svc/$metadata

entry.title: Please let me know how to know what each component name in BPs indicate SAP module/solution name?
entry.content:
There are the following component names in Best Practices scope item.

Please let me know what each component name indicates SAP module/solution (FI/SD/MM/PP...etc).

I think a list such as  "component name" - "SAP module/Solution name".

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:

My name is Hang from Vietnam. I had worked as a SAP consultant in PP module for over 10 years and now I'm working as a new ABAP developer.

I would really appreciate getting a free SAP certification because it provide me an advantage in my job.

Please kindly consider my application.

Have a nice week.

Thank you very much!

entry.title: SAP CDC REST API Error - Phone number is not whitelisted
entry.content:
SAP Customer Data Cloud (Gigya) REST API accounts.tfa.phone.sendVerificationCode is throwing an error as given below,

{
"callId": "b0e7a2c7***********2b6eb6ab42",
"errorCode": 400030,
"errorDetails": "Phone number is not whitelisted",
"errorMessage": "Operation not allowed",
"apiVersion": 2,
"statusCode": 400,
"statusReason": "Bad Request",
"time": "2024-06-10T05:57:35.323Z"
}

The site uses extension as SMS provider (no out of the box provider user). The TFA was attempted using gigya screens.

How to whitelist the number or resolve this issue?

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: That's good news, even though I now mainly use Eclipse for coding. Where I still need the SAP GUI is the creation/adaption of "old" dynpros/screens and that's really no fun with SAP GUI Java. Therefore, the missing graphical layout editor would be the most important feature for me. 🙂
